ety: Prk. मूसलं; S. मुसलं

more: n. m. Dim. mūslī , n. f. S. मुसल, , Pālī, musalo . 1. A long heavy wooden pestle with which grain is beaten in a mortar. 1. Apnā ghar sanjhaut na, ankā ghar mūsar aisan bātī . Prov. Bhoj. In her own house not even a farthing rush-light, in another's house a wick she'll burn as a pestle thick. 2. Chhatrī bhagat, na mūsal dhanvī . Nor Chhatrī anchorite, nor bow of rammer made. (Can the Ethiopian change his skin?). 3. Hāl meṅ phāl, dahī meṅ mūsal . Prov. A share in the plough, a pestle in tyre. (Said to an impertinent intruder). 2. The penis. Hamāre mūsar se. mūslā-dhār , n. m. Heavy rain. mūslā-dhār barasnā , v. n. To rain in torrents; to rain cats and dogs. mūsloṅ ḍhol bajānā , v. a. To proclaim by beat of drum. mūs'lā , n. m. A rod; piston; bolt.
s. m. A wooden pestle. موسلان سون ڐهول بجانا mūslāṅ sūṅ ḍhol bajānā (dakh.) To feast, to make merry.
subs: or mūsal-dhārā, s.m. = mūslā-dhār, and musal-dhār, qq.v.

To meet with a difficulty. mushkil-pasand , One who delights in using big words. mushkil se , adv. 1. With difficulty. 2. Hardly; scarcely. Mushkil se das ser hogā. mushkil se nikālnā , v. a. n. m. A blow with the fist. Drisṭ na dīkhe, mushṭ na āve, kahe se ko patiāë re? Jo kahiye hai nāhīṅ, hai so kaheo na jāë re . Kabīr. Nor eye can see, nor touch can feel, on whom do you believe? Whate'er you say He's not, and what He is who can conceive?

def: part. adj. Increased, augmented; additional; more; — s.m. A kind of poem composed of rhyming verses (after the manner of the g̠azal), each hemistich of which is followed by some additional words in prose, which may also rhyme (e.g. Sun Soz! ʻabas̤ dekhke, ḥairān hogā, Khūbān-kā jamāl; Dil zulf-meṅ uljhegā, pareshān hogā, Mat le yěh wabāl: Yěh ćāl burī hai, tujh-se nibhne-kī nahīṅ, Á mān kahā; Kyā haṅstā hai? tū bahut pashemān hogā, Mat dāṅt nikāl. — Saudā. The merit of these poems is supposed to consist in the fact that it is possible to read them with or without the short additional verses, such as Khūbān-kā jamāl, &c. in the above specimen).
more: n. m. A kind of verse of which every line has a shorter one attached to it.

(part. pass. X. of زيد Heb. ) 1. adj. Increased. 2. s. m. The name of a kind of verse, in which every line has a shorter one annexed to it (as the following from Mīr Soz:-- سن سوزعبث ديکهکي حيران هوگا خوبان کا جمال دل زلف مين الجهيگا پريشان هوگا مت لي يہہ وبال يہہ چال بري هي تجهہ سي نبهنيکي نہين آمان کہا کياهنستاهي توبہت پشيمان هوگا مت دانت نکال Sun Soz! aﻌbas̤ dekhke, ḥairāṅ hogā, ḵẖūbāṅ kā jamāl; dil zulf meṅ uljhegā, pareshāṅ hogā, mat le yih wabāl. Yih chāl burī hai, tujh se nibhne kī nahīṅ, ā mān kahā; kyā haṅstā hai? tū bahut pashemāṅ hogā, mat dānt nikāl. Hear, Soz! by idly looking on the beauty of the fair thou wilt be involved in perplexity; thy heart will be entangled in their tresses and much distressed; do not encounter this trouble. This is a dangerous course, which thou canst not pursue with safety. Come then, listen to my words. Why laughest thou? Do not shew thy teeth, th…

def: s.m. Urine
more: n. m. मूत्र, Pālī, muttam . 1. Urine (پيشاب). 2. Semen. 3. A son. 1. Yāhī paiṅḍe pūt hai, yāhī paiṅḍe mūt, Nām līyā to pūt hai, nahīṅ mūt kā mūt . Dohā The same way comes a son, the same way semen's passed, A good son is a son, or semen merely cast. 2. Shāyad kisī Aṅgrez kā tū mūt hai, laṛke! Har bāt meṅ hoṭoṅ pe tere 'ḍām bagar' hai . Some Englishman's vile sperm you sure must be, With every word 'd—n b—r' you mix free. mūt aṭaknā , v. n. To have strangury. mūt-pātr , n. m. 1. A chamber pot. 2. To piss through fear.
Sant. n. f. 1. A bundle ( gaṭhṛī ). 2. See charas. moṭ'iyā , n. m. A porter; cooly.

s.m. pl. (obl. pl. in A.) of mutaʻāhid, q.v.

ety: for A. متعينة mutaʻaiyanat, fem. of mutaʻaiyan, pass. part. of تعيّن; see mutaʻaiyin
def: part. adj. = mutaʻaiyin, q.v.; — s.m. An appointment, a station, post, command; establishment.
more: s. m. A station, post, command, an appointment, establishment.

ety: act. part. of تعين 'to be appointed, or prescribed, or assigned,' v. of عين 'to raise the eye to'; but quasi-pass. of عيّن (ii of عين
def: part. adj. Appointed; assigned; constituted; deputed; fixed, determined; certain; — s.m. A deputy, locum-tenens; an agent
more: adj. Appointed; constituted. mutaaiyun k. , v. a. To appoint (to an office). mutaaiyun h. , v. n. To fill an office.
(part. act. V. from عين Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Appointed, fixed, constituted, deputed, determined; s. m. Deputy, agent. متعين کرنا mutaʼaﻌiyin karna, To appoint, depute, &c.
subs: v.t. To appoint (a person, to, or over, -par, an office); to depute

ety: act. part. of تعجّب, v of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Full of admiration (for, -se), wondering (at); astonished, amazed; — wonderful, strange
more: (part. act. V. from عجب) adj. Full of admiration, admiring, wondering, astonished, amazed.
subs: (-se), To wonder or marvel (at), to be astonished (at), &c.

def: (oblique pl. of mutaʼaḵẖḵẖir, act. part. of تأخّر 'to go back, to become behind or last'; quasi pass. of أخّر 'to put back,' &c. ii of rt أخر), adj. & s.m. pl. Of the later or more modern times; late, modern; — the moderns.

ety: act. part. of تعاقب 'to follow each other by turns; to alternate'; vi of عقب 'to follow; succeed'
def: part. adj. Following; successive; pursuing; subsequent; — adv. Afterwards, after.
more: (part. act. VI. of عقب Heb. ) 1. adj. Successive, following, pursuing. 2. adv. Afterwards, after.

def: (act. part. of تعقّد 'to become tied or bound,' &c., v of عقد 'to tie,' &c.), Tied, bound, fastened together; consolidated; concreted.
more: (part. act. V. of عقد) adj. Bound, tied or bound together, consolidated.

ety: for A. متعالٍ mutaʻāliṉ, act. part. of تعالی, vi of (and syn. with
def: adj. High, exalted, sublime.
more: (for متعالي part. act. VI. of علو Heb. ) adj. High, sublime.

ety: for A. متعالٍ mutaʻāliṉ, act. part. of تعالی, vi of (and syn. with
def: adj. High, exalted, sublime.

ety: act. part. of تألف, v of ألف 'to be familiar or intimate' (with
def: part. adj. Familiar, intimate; associated, conjoined; — s.m. One who cultivates the acquaintance (of), one who conciliates the good-will (of).

ety: act. part. of تعلّق, v of (and syn. with
def: s.m. Hanging (to), suspended (to), depending (from); — belonging (to); depending (upon), dependent (upon); connected (with), attached (to); annexed, appended, appendant; subjoined; sticking close (to); occupied or engaged (in); — appertaining (to), touching, concerning, relative (to); pertinent, relevant, applicable; — addicted (to); dedicated (to); — a kinsman; dependant; — an appendage
more: adj. علق hung to. 1. Connected; annexed; appended; subjoined. 2. Depending on; dependant. 3. Appertaining; concerning ( sambaṅdhī ). 4. Pertinent; relevant; applicable. mutaalliq-i-zāt-i-khās , adj. Personal; private. mutaalliq-i-fel ; S. क्रियाविशेषण ( Gram .) Adverb. mutaalliq k. , v. a. 1. To append; join. 2. To extend to; apply; make applicable. 3. To attribute; ascribe; impute. 4. To assign; make over to; to entrust; invest. mutaalliq moharrir yā mutasaddī . Clerical. mutaaliqāt', mutaalliqān, mutaalliqīn , n. m. 1. Children; family. 2. Domestics; dependents.

(part. act. V. of علق) adj. 1. Hanging, suspended, depending. 2. Belonging to, appendant, concerning, relative to. 3. Connected with, attached to, addicted, occupied or engaged (in). s. Appendage. Plur. Pers. متعلقان mutaʼaﻌlliḳān, or Arab. متعلقات mutaʼaﻌlliḳāt, s. m. Domesticks, children, family, appendages, possessions. متعلق هونا mutaʼaﻌlliḳ konā, To depend.
subs: 'What is joined to a verb,' an adverb
v.t. To append; to join (to); to extend (to), to make applicable (to), to apply (to); to attribute, ascribe, impute; to assign, make over (to), entrust (to)
adj. Pertaining to a clerk or clerks; clerical.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُتَعَلِّق (muta'alliq), from Arabic مُتَعَلِّق (mutaʕalliq).

def: adverb
wety: From مُتَعَلِّق (muta'alliq) + فِعْل (fe'l) with ezafe.

ety: for A. متعلقة mutaʻalliqat, fem. of mutaʻalliq
def: adj. & s.m. = mutaʻalliq, q.v.
subs: adj. Pertaining to a family or household, family.

def: s.m. pl. (of mutaʻalliq), Children, family; domestics, dependants.
s.m. pl. (of mutaʻalliqa, q.v.), Dependencies, appurtenances; circumstances (of a case); appendages; possessions, belongings; — children, family; domestics, dependants.
(obl. pl. of mutaʻalliq), s.m. pl. = mutaʻalliqān, q.v. s.m. pl. (of mutaʻalliq), Children, family; domestics, dependants.

ety: act. part. of تألّم, v of ألم 'to be in pain,' &c.
def: part. adj. Afflicted, pained, grieved, vexed, troubled, sad; discontented.

ety: act. part. of تعلّم 'to be taught; to learn'; v of علم 'to know,' and quasi-pass. of علّم 'to teach,' ii of علم
def: part. adj. & s.m. Taught, instructed; — applying oneself to learn, learning; — a learner, scholar, disciple; — a teacher.
more: (part. pass. V. from علم) 1. adj. Being taught, learning. 2. (subst.) A scholar, disciple. ¶ mutaʼaﻌllim (part. act.) Teaching.
